body.u: Looking out of the window at the children walking down the street makes me remember my past years especially from leaving school. After spending two years on a cermercialcommercial course at school I finally left 1980 it must have been yes. I was thrilled to bits one morning to find they had accepted me in a firm a few yards down the road I was to be a junior secretary/clerk I’ve never ran down the passage so fast. The first day was the best getting to noknow all the different people and places to go. I’d always wanted to work there and finally to my amazement after being there for two years I finally got promotion to personal secretary/clerk to the general manager. That Christmas the lads from the tool makers yard came to our party that’s where I met Keith my husband he was so nice and kind. We went out together for about two years and then got engaged. We were the happiest couple any one could imagine we started to put some money away for when we got married then finally the big day came May 13th 1985 we got married I was in a lovely white long dress with a headdress Minster Abbey at 3.00 we had professional photographs the bridesmaids (6 of them) they were a beautiful shade of orange. 1986 I left my works to work at an estate agency personal manager’s secretary it was a big estate agencies.

About a year after this my sister finally got engaged and within a year she was married. I’ll always remember it because it was the middle of Autumn the colours were so preatypretty. 1990 we packed our jobs in and with the children Cheryl 1 year old Trisham 3 years old we finally got the house of our dreams a big house with a big garden. Keith and I went into our own bussnessbusiness and it had prospered ever since we are both near to retirement the children have left and got children but I’m happy for them. I’ve been married thirty seven years and we are still the happiest people around. The children grew up very quickly and know have children. Keith’s and mine grandchildren Paul and Claire we are proud of them.
There they go down the street they look like Paul and Claire. “Oh well Keith make a cup of tea love”
